concerns.text: Task T010 (Ingest) references a hardcoded URL to 'numenta/NAB' which is unrelated to molecular UV-Vis data. This task cannot produce the artifact (clean CSV) required by T012 (Split) and T017 (Model). The data-flow is broken at the source; T010 must be corrected to fetch from the verified UV-Vis ML dataset or PubChem/SDBS as per spec.md FR-001 before any downstream tasks can execute.

FR-007 requires detecting collinearity AND masking attribution weights for redundant subgraphs. T024 performs detection, but T025 (Explain) is the consumer of the 'redundancy masks'. The current ordering implies T025 runs independently or after T024 without explicit data dependency. T025 must explicitly consume the output of T024 (the mask flags) to satisfy the 'masking' requirement of FR-007. Without this explicit flow, the masking logic may be skipped.

Task T027 (Analyze Results) aggregates 'power status' and 'redundancy masks' into metrics.json. However, T020 (Power Analysis) and T024 (Collinearity Check) are the producers of these specific artifacts. T027 is listed in Phase 5 but does not explicitly state it depends on the completion of T020 and T024. If T027 runs before T020/T024 complete, the final artifact will be incomplete. The dependency chain must be explicit.

Task T013 (Add chunked loading) is listed as a separate task from T010 (Ingest). Since chunking is an implementation detail of the ingestion logic required to meet the 7GB RAM constraint (Plan.md), splitting them into separate tasks creates a false dependency where T013 might be completed without T010 actually implementing the logic. These should be merged or T013 should be a sub-task of T010 to ensure the artifact (ingest.py) is complete and functional.
